explanation:
**Technical calculations for AI:** token costs, context windows, model selection. Decisions that scale.

## How It Works

This calculator applies pricing formulas with your input values, comparing total monthly costs and capabilities across Claude, Gemini, and OpenAI models. It helps you understand API spending based on your token usage patterns.

## Final Notes

This calculation is for reference purposes. For critical decisions (technical, financial), consult with a specialist. Prices are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What's the difference between Claude and Gemini?
faq.a: Claude (Anthropic) excels at long-form reasoning, writing, and complex analysis. Gemini (Google) is strong in multimodal tasks like image and video understanding.

faq.q: What's the context window and why does it matter?
faq.a: The context window is the maximum amount of text a model can process at once. Larger windows support longer conversations and documents. Claude Sonnet: 200k tokens, Gemini Pro: 2M tokens, GPT-4o: 128k tokens.

faq.q: How much does Claude Sonnet cost?
faq.a: Claude Sonnet pricing (2026): $3/M input tokens, $15/M output tokens.

faq.q: What's the price difference between Claude Sonnet and Opus?
faq.a: Claude Opus is more capable but pricier (2026): $15/M input tokens, $75/M output tokens vs. Sonnet's $3/$15.
Gemini Pro pricing (2026): $3.50/M input tokens, $10.50/M output tokens.

faq.q: How do I choose the right model for my project?
faq.a: Choose Claude for reasoning and writing tasks, Gemini for multimodal work (images/video), GPT-4o for balanced general-purpose performance. Use this calculator to compare costs for your expected token volume.

faq.q: Are there free AI model alternatives?
faq.a: Yes. Llama 3 via Groq and Mistral offer free tiers with rate limits. Google also provides free tier access to Gemini with limitations.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free with no registration or payment required.
